text: I'll start by reading the key skills and locating the files in question.

input.description: Read project-git skill and list Picard dir

data.content:
---
name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.
---LS---

data.content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.SerreTwist
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotFunctorDef
9	
10	/-!
11	# Projective morphisms carrying a very ample line bundle
12	
13	Mathlib v4.31 has no projective-morphism class and no (very) ampleness
14	vocabulary.  Following the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118` (comment
15	`C-0002`), this file defines:
16	
17	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf n₀ S m` — the Serre twist `O(m)` on the
18	  relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`, the pullback of
19	  `ProjTwist.serreTwist` from the integral model along `toProjInt`;
20	* `ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange` — `O(m)` commutes with base
21	  change of the ambient projective space;
22	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith π L` — the **projective-with-`L`** predicate:
23	  `π : X ⟶ S` factors through a closed immersion `i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)`
24	  over `S` with `L ≅ i^* O(1)`;
25	
26	with the stability facts the Quot-scheme endgame consumes:
27	
28	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.isProper` — projective morphisms are proper;
29	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.comp_isClosedImmersion` — a closed immersion
30	  into a projective scheme is projective (carrying the restricted bundle);
31	* `Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith.baseChange` — stability under base change
32	  (carrying the pulled-back bundle).
33	
34	Everything is at `Scheme.{0}`: the Serre twist rests on the descent engine
35	`Scheme.Modules.glue`, which is universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}`
36	(`GlueDescent.lean:934`).
37	
38	Blueprint: `def:twisting_sheaf`, `def:projective_with`,
39	`lem:projective_with_proper`, `lem:projective_with_closed_immersion`,
40	`lem:projective_with_base_change`
41	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
42	-/
43	
44	open CategoryTheory Limits MvPolynomial
45	
46	noncomputable section
47	
48	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
49	
50	namespace ProjectiveSpace
51	
52	variable (n₀ : Type) (S : Scheme.{0})
53	
54	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the relative projective space `ℙ(n₀; S)`:
55	the pullback of the glued twisting sheaf on the integral model. -/
56	def twistingSheaf (m : ℕ) : (ℙ(n₀; S)).Modules :=
57	  (Scheme.Modules.pullback (toProjInt n₀ S)).obj (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
58	
59	variable {S} {S' : Scheme.{0}}
60	
61	/-- The Serre twist commutes with base change of the ambient projective
62	space: `(map g)^* O(m) ≅ O(m)`. -/
63	def twistingSheafBaseChange (g : S' ⟶ S) (m : ℕ) :
64	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (map n₀ g)).obj (twistingSheaf n₀ S m) ≅
65	      twistingSheaf n₀ S' m :=
66	  Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (map_toProjInt n₀ g) (ProjTwist.serreTwist n₀ m)
67	
68	end ProjectiveSpace
69	
70	/-- `π : X ⟶ S` is a **projective morphism carrying the line bundle `L`**
71	([Nitsure] §5, [EGA II] 5.5): there are a closed immersion
72	`i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin (d+1); S)` over `S` and an isomorphism of `L` with the
73	pullback of the Serre twist `O(1)`.  This is the faithful encoding of
74	"projective with relatively very ample `L`" settled in inbox `I-0118`. -/
75	def Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith {X S : Scheme.{0}} (π : X ⟶ S) (L : X.Modules) :
76	    Prop :=
77	  ∃ (d : ℕ) (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)),
78	    IsClosedImmersion i ∧ i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π ∧
79	      Nonempty (L ≅ (Scheme.Modules.pullback i).obj
80	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1))
81	
82	namespace Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
83	
84	variable {X S : Scheme.{0}} {π : X ⟶ S} {L : X.Modules}
85	
86	/-- **Projective morphisms are proper**: a closed immersion is proper, the
87	structural morphism of projective space is proper, and properness is stable
88	under composition. -/
89	theorem isProper (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) : IsProper π := by
90	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, -⟩ := h
91	  haveI := hi
92	  rw [← hcomp]
93	  infer_instance
94	
95	/-- **Composition with a closed immersion**: if `π` is projective carrying
96	`L` and `j` is a closed immersion into `X`, then `j ≫ π` is projective
97	carrying `j^* L`. -/
98	theorem comp_isClosedImmersion (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {Y : Scheme.{0}}
99	    (j : Y ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion j] :
100	    (j ≫ π).IsProjectiveWith ((Scheme.Modules.pullback j).obj L) := by
101	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
102	  haveI := hi
103	  refine ⟨d, j ≫ i, inferInstance, by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp], ?_⟩
104	  exact ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫
105	    Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (rfl : j ≫ i = j ≫ i)
106	      (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)⟩
107	
108	/-- The comparison morphism from the base-changed total space into the
109	base-changed projective space. -/
110	private def baseChangeLift {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) {d : ℕ}
111	    (i : X ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S)) (hcomp : i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S) = π) :
112	    pullback π g ⟶ ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') :=
113	  (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).lift
114	    (pullback.fst π g ≫ i) (pullback.snd π g)
115	    (by rw [Category.assoc, hcomp, pullback.condition])
116	
117	/-- **Stability under base change**: if `π : X ⟶ S` is projective carrying
118	`L` and `g : S' ⟶ S`, then the base change `X ×_S S' ⟶ S'` is projective
119	carrying the pullback of `L`. -/
120	theorem baseChange (h : π.IsProjectiveWith L) {S' : Scheme.{0}} (g : S' ⟶ S) :
121	    (pullback.snd π g).IsProjectiveWith
122	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).obj L) := by
123	  obtain ⟨d, i, hi, hcomp, ⟨e⟩⟩ := h
124	  haveI := hi
125	  refine ⟨d, baseChangeLift g i hcomp, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
126	  · -- the comparison square exhibits the lift as the base change of `i`
127	    have h1 : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S')
128	        = pullback.snd π g := IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
129	    have hsq : IsPullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp) (pullback.fst π g)
130	        (ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g) i := by
131	      have hbig : IsPullback
132	          (baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S') ↘ S'))
133	          (pullback.fst π g) g (i ≫ (ℙ(Fin (d + 1); S) ↘ S)) := by
134	        rw [h1, hcomp]
135	        exact (IsPullback.of_hasPullback π g).flip
136	      exact IsPullback.of_right hbig
137	        (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _)
138	        (ProjectiveSpace.isPullback_map (Fin (d + 1)) g).flip
139	    exact MorphismProperty.of_isPullback hsq.flip hi
140	  · exact IsPullback.lift_snd _ _ _ _
141	  · refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π g)).mapIso e ≪≫
142	      Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso (IsPullback.lift_fst _ _ _ _ :
143	        baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
144	          = pullback.fst π g ≫ i).symm
145	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1) ≪≫ ?_⟩
146	    -- collapse `(lift ≫ map)^* O(1)` to `lift^* (map^* O(1))`, then use the
147	    -- base-change isomorphism of the twist
148	    exact (Scheme.pullbackTriangleIso
149	        (rfl : baseChangeLift g i hcomp ≫ ProjectiveSpace.map (Fin (d + 1)) g
150	          = _) (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheaf (Fin (d + 1)) S 1)).symm ≪≫
151	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (baseChangeLift g i hcomp)).mapIso
152	        (ProjectiveSpace.twistingSheafBaseChange (Fin (d + 1)) g 1)
153	
154	end Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith
155	
156	end AlgebraicGeometry
157

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.ProjectiveMorphism
8	
9	/-!
10	# Serre finiteness for the section graded module (statement)
11	
12	The deep remaining input of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
13	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`, inbox `I-0109`): for a projective scheme over
14	a field carrying a very ample line bundle `L` and a coherent sheaf `F`, the
15	section graded ring `R(X, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian, the section
16	graded module `M(X, F, L) = ⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module over it,
17	and each graded component is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector space.
18	
19	This file gives the **honest statement** against the projectivity vocabulary
20	of `Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean` (the `IsProjectiveWith` closed-immersion
21	predicate — very ampleness in the encoding settled in inbox `I-0118`), as a
22	named typed `sorry` leaf: the proof is Serre's theorem ([Hartshorne] II.5.17
23	ff., [Nitsure] §1) and is genuinely deep (multi-session; it needs the
24	pushforward-to-projective-space reduction and cohomological finiteness on
25	`ℙⁿ`).
26	
27	Compared with the blueprint node, which hypothesises an *ample* `L` and
28	passes to a Veronese power, the Lean statement hypothesises the very ample
29	form directly (`IsProjectiveWith`) — this is the form in which the
30	Quot-scheme endgame ([Nitsure] §5) consumes it.
31	
32	Blueprint: `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`
33	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
34	-/
35	
36	open CategoryTheory Limits
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	/-- The structural `κ`-algebra structure on the global sections of a scheme
43	over `Spec κ` (the absolute analogue of `Scheme.Hom.fiberResidueMap`). -/
44	def Scheme.Hom.structuralSectionsHom {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [CommRing κ]
45	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) : κ →+* Γ(X, ⊤) :=
46	  ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of κ)).inv ≫ π.appTop).hom
47	
48	/-- **Serre finiteness for the section graded module**
49	(`lem:sectionGradedModule_fg`; [Nitsure] §1, [Hartshorne] II.5.17 ff.):
50	for `π : X ⟶ Spec κ` projective carrying the very ample line bundle `L`
51	and a coherent (finitely presented) sheaf `F` on `X`,
52	
53	1. the section graded ring `⊕ₘ Γ(X, L^{⊗m})` is Noetherian;
54	2. the section graded module `⊕ₘ Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite module
55	   over it;
56	3. each component `Γ(X, F ⊗ L^{⊗m})` is a finite-dimensional `κ`-vector
57	   space (via the structural `κ`-action).
58	
59	NAMED LEAF (`sorry`): the geometric heart of the Hilbert-polynomial lane
60	(inbox `I-0109`), awaiting the pushforward-to-`ℙⁿ` reduction and
61	cohomological finiteness of coherent sheaves on projective space. -/
62	theorem sectionGradedModule_fg {X : Scheme.{0}} {κ : Type} [Field κ]
63	    (π : X ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of κ)) (L F : X.Modules)
64	    (hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L) (hF : F.IsFinitePresentation) :
65	    (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
66	     IsNoetherianRing (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L)))
67	    ∧ (letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedRing_gsemiring L
68	       letI := Scheme.Modules.sectionGradedModule_gmodule F L
69	       Module.Finite (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.sectionDeg L))
70	         (DirectSum ℕ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L)))
71	    ∧ ∀ m : ℕ,
72	        letI := Module.compHom (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m)
73	          π.structuralSectionsHom
74	        FiniteDimensional κ (Scheme.Modules.moduleSectionDeg F L m) := by
75	  sorry
76	
77	end AlgebraicGeometry
78

text: Now the blueprint section and the fg lemma.

data.content:
320	  \(X_s\) (\cref{def:fiber_module_restriction}), the twist
321	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) is the \(m\)-twist of
322	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\) by \(L_s\) on \(X_s\) (\cref{def:sheafModuleTwist}), and
323	  the dimension is taken with respect to the \(\kappa(s)\)-vector-space
324	  structure on sections over the fibre (\cref{def:fiber_sections_module}).
325	  This is exactly the graded Hilbert function of the section graded module
326	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}); it uses
327	  only global sections \(H^0\).
328	\end{definition}
329	
330	\begin{lemma}
331	[Finite generation of the section graded module]
332	  \label{lem:sectionGradedModule_fg}
333	  \leanok
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionGradedModule_fg}
335	  \uses{def:sectionGradedModule, def:sectionGradedRing, def:projective_with}
336	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Stratification by Hilbert
337	  % Polynomials" (read from
338	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L453-L464).
339	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
340	  % "Recall that if $F$ is a coherent sheaf on
341	  %  $X$ whose support is proper over $k$, then
342	  %  the \textbf{Hilbert polynomial} ${\Phi}\in \Q[\lambda]$ of $F$ is defined by
343	  %  the function
344	  %  $${\Phi}(m) = \chi(F(m)) =
345	  %  \sum_{i=0}^n (-1)^i \dim_k H^i(X, F\otimes L^{\otimes m})$$
346	  %  where the dimensions of the cohomologies are finite because of the
347	  %  coherence and properness conditions."
348	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1 (FGA Explained Ch.~5); Serre's theorem on
349	  ample line bundles, [Hartshorne], I.7 / II.5.}
350	  Let \(X_s\) be projective over the field \(\kappa(s)\) carrying the very
351	  ample line bundle \(L_s\) (\cref{def:projective_with}: a closed immersion
352	  \(X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\) with
353	  \(L_s \cong \mathcal{O}(1)|_{X_s}\)), and let \(\mathcal{F}_s\) be a
354	  coherent sheaf on \(X_s\).
355	  Then the section graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedRing}) is a
356	  Noetherian graded ring, and the section graded module
357	  \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) (\cref{def:sectionGradedModule}) is a finitely
358	  generated graded \(R(X_s, L_s)\)-module. In particular each component
359	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a finite-dimensional
360	  \(\kappa(s)\)-vector space.
361	  (For an ample \(L_s\) the same conclusions follow by passing to a very
362	  ample Veronese power; the very ample form stated here is the one consumed
363	  by the Quot-scheme construction.)
364	\end{lemma}
365	
366	\begin{proof}
367	  \uses{def:sectionGradedRing, def:sectionGradedModule}
368	  The closed immersion \(i : X_s \hookrightarrow \mathbb{P}^N_{\kappa(s)}\)
369	  with \(L_s \cong i^* \mathcal{O}(1)\) reduces the claims to projective
370	  space: pushing forward along \(i\) identifies
371	  \(\Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) with
372	  \(\Gamma(\mathbb{P}^N, (i_* \mathcal{F}_s)(m))\), and \(i_* \mathcal{F}_s\)
373	  is coherent. By Serre's theorem the section
374	  graded ring \(R(X_s, L_s)\) is a finitely generated graded
375	  \(\kappa(s)\)-algebra (a quotient of the polynomial ring in \(N+1\)
376	  variables in large degrees), so it is Noetherian. For the coherent sheaf
377	  \(\mathcal{F}_s\), Serre's theorem yields that
378	  \(\bigoplus_{m \ge 0} \Gamma(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m})\) is a
379	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists

290	            rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])) :=
291	      pullbackUnitIso_trans_symm_eqToIso
292	        (show (glueData n).f i i = (glueData n).t i i ≫ (glueData n).f i i by
293	          rw [(glueData n).t_id i, Category.id_comp])
294	
295	/-- **(C2)** The triple-overlap cocycle condition for the Serre-twist
296	transitions, in the exact form consumed by `Scheme.Modules.glue`.  At the
297	level of transition units this is the identity
298	`(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m (Xⱼ/Xₖ)^m = (Xᵢ/Xₖ)^m` in `Γ(V(i,j,k), O)`; the remaining work is
299	transporting the conjugated scalar isomorphisms to the triple overlap through
300	the `pullbackUnitIso` coherences (the rank-one analogue of
301	`bundleTransition_cocycle_transport`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`). -/
302	lemma twistTransition_cocycle (m : ℕ) (i j k : n) :
303	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
304	        (pullback.fst ((glueData n).f i j) ((glueData n).f i k)) ((glueData n).f i j)
305	        ((glueData n).t i j ≫ (glueData n).f j i) (twistTransition n m i j) ≪≫
306	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
307	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid (glueData n) i j k)).app
308	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U j).ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
309	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
310	        ((glueData n).t' i j k ≫ pullback.fst ((glueData n).f j k) ((glueData n).f j i))
311	        ((glueData n).f j k) ((glueData n).t j k ≫ (glueData n).f k j)
312	        (twistTransition n m j k) ≪≫
313	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
314	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt (glueData n) i j k)).app
315	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U k).ringCatSheaf)
316	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr
317	        (Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src (glueData n) i j k)).app
318	          (SheafOfModules.unit ((glueData n).U i).ringCatSheaf) ≪≫
319	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport
320	        (pullback.snd ((glueData n).f i j) ((glueData n).f i k)) ((glueData n).f i k)
321	        ((glueData n).t i k ≫ (glueData n).f k i) (twistTransition n m i k) := by
322	  sorry
323	
324	/-! ## The Serre twisting sheaf
325	
326	The descent engine `Scheme.Modules.glue` (`Picard/GlueDescent.lean`) is
327	universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}` (see the NOTE at
328	`GlueDescent.lean:934`), so the glued sheaf itself is only available for a
329	`Type 0` index — which covers the intended consumers `n = Fin (m + 1)`
330	(`ℙ^m`).  The cover, units and transitions above stay universe-polymorphic
331	for a future generalisation of the engine. -/
332	
333	section Universe0
334	
335	variable (n₀ : Type)
336	
337	/-- The Serre twist `O(m)` on the glued total space of the basic-open cover. -/
338	def serreTwistGlued (m : ℕ) : (glueData n₀).glued.Modules :=
339	  Scheme.Modules.glue (glueData n₀)

===TASK YAML===
id: T14
project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
objective: 'Build the ample / very-ample line-bundle and projective-morphism foundation
  that Mathlib v4.31 lacks. This is the SINGLE shared prerequisite blocking QuotScheme
  representability, curve projectivity, and the Picard-scheme root instHasPicScheme
  (see inbox I-0109 ampleness gap and I-0118 statement trap). It has NO prerequisite
  and unblocks T15. Do NOT touch the FGA assembly or attempt QuotScheme representability
  here — build only the foundation.


  MATHLIB v4.31 ASSETS TO BUILD ON (verified present): AlgebraicGeometry.Proj (ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.lean);
  Proj.toSpecZero with instance IsProper for finite-type graded algebras (ProjectiveSpectrum/Proper.lean:368),
  LocallyOfFiniteType (:136), IsSeparated (:82); Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:439)
  and Proj.map functoriality (Functor.lean:144); MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule
  / MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra (RingTheory/MvPolynomial/Homogeneous.lean:608); AffineSpace
  pattern (AlgebraicGeometry/AffineSpace.lean:44); SheafOfModules.unit. Verified ABSENT
  (must be built): any IsProjective/QuasiProjective morphism class, VeryAmple/IsAmple
  for sheaves, relative Proj / P^n_S over a base, Serre twisting sheaf O(1).


  DELIVERABLES (new sorry-free Lean files or honestly named-leaf-factored, each with
  a complete-proof blueprint node):

  1. Relative projective space P(n;S) := pullback (terminal.from S) (terminal.from
  (Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin (n+1)) Int))), in the AffineSpace.lean:44
  style; derive proper + locallyOfFiniteType + separated structural morphism by base
  change from the Mathlib Proj instances.

  2. Serre twist O(1) (and O(m)) as a SheafOfModules on Proj 𝒜, glued over the awayι
  / basic-open affine cover with transition t_i/t_j — REUSE the project module-gluing
  engine (Picard/GlueDescent.lean, GrassmannianZariskiSheaf.lean, Scheme.Modules.glue).

  3. A projective-with-L predicate: pi : X ⟶ S is projective carrying L when there
  is a closed immersion i : X ↪ P(n;S) over S with L ≅ i^* O(1). Prove stability facts
  the endgame consumes: base change, composition with closed immersions, and that
  it implies IsProper.

  4. Serre finiteness / boundedness: for pi proper with L relatively ample, the section
  graded module ⊕_m Γ(X_s, F_s ⊗ L_s^⊗m) is finitely generated over ⊕_m Γ(X_s, L_s^⊗m)
  (blueprint lem:sectionGradedModule_fg, I-0109, the deep remaining Hilbert-lane input)
  and the uniform Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity bound (blueprint lem:quot_boundedness).
  The DirectSum-decomposition bridge to gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational (already
  sorry-free) is documented in I-0109.


  Verify each file with lake build (or lake env lean) + lean_verify axiom checks.
  Prefer axiom-clean; where a step is genuinely too deep, factor a NAMED typed sorry
  with an honest statement + blueprint node. Coordinate blueprint nodes with the existing
  Picard_QuotScheme.tex / Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex chapters.'

1	## Summary
2	
3	T14's projectivity foundation is built and committed (`5fe44977a3`): four new Lean files land relative projective space ℙ(n;S) with a proper structural morphism (axiom-clean), the Serre twist O(m) on `Proj ℤ[Xᵢ]` glued through the project descent engine, the projective-with-L predicate with all three stability facts fully proved, and the honest statement of Serre finiteness for the section graded module. Exactly **two named sorries** remain in the new material, both flagged in blueprint/inbox: the C2 gluing cocycle (`twistTransition_cocycle`) and the intended deep leaf `sectionGradedModule_fg`. Full project build is green (8666 jobs); the blueprint DAG parses with 0 dangling references. The `quotBoundedness` statement was deliberately deferred (see Issues).
4	
5	## Progress
6	- Picard/ProjectiveSpace.lean: NEW, axiom-clean — ℙ(n;S) as pullback over Spec ℤ (AffineSpace pattern), gradeZeroRingEquiv, isProper_over/isSeparated_over by base change from Mathlib's Proj.toSpecZero, map/map_id/map_comp/isPullback_map.
7	- Picard/SerreTwist.lean: NEW — O(m) glued from D₊(Xᵢ) trivializations with unit transitions (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m; awayUnit/overlapUnit and C1 proved; 1 sorry = twistTransition_cocycle (C2 transport).
8	- Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean: NEW, proofs complete — twistingSheaf on ℙ(n;S) + base-change iso, Scheme.Hom.IsProjectiveWith, isProper/comp_isClosedImmersion/baseChange (sorryAx only via the O(1) term in statements).
9	- Picard/SerreFiniteness.lean: NEW — sectionGradedModule_fg stated (Noetherian ring ∧ Module.Finite ∧ componentwise FiniteDimensional, hypothesis IsProjectiveWith); 1 intended sorry.
10	- blueprint/Picard_QuotScheme.tex: new section sec:projective_vocabulary (12 nodes, complete proofs); lem:sectionGradedModule_fg flipped to statement-\leanok with very-ample hypothesis; archon:covers updated; DAG parses (2656 nodes, 0 dangling).
11	- AlgebraicJacobian.lean: imports the four new modules; full lake build green.
12	- Coordination: inbox I-0125 (status + caveats for Ground); memory t14-projectivity-foundation.md (state + v4.31 recipes); commit 5fe44977a3.
13	
14	## Issues
15	- `Scheme.Modules.glue`/`glueLift` are universe-monomorphic at `Scheme.GlueData.{0}` (GlueDescent.lean:934), so O(m), the predicate and stability facts are pinned to `Scheme.{0}`; the future QuotScheme restatement will specialize to universe 0 unless the engine is generalized.
16	- `twistTransition_cocycle` (C2) is the single sorry gating axiom-cleanliness of everything mentioning `serreTwist`; it needs `pullbackUnitIso` comp-coherence, a scalar-atom pullback-naturality lemma, and `awayToSection` restriction compatibility — one focused session (mirror of the Grassmannian `bundleTransition_cocycle_transport`).
17	- `TODO.quotBoundedness` (deliverable 4b) was NOT stated: a faithful Lean statement needs Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity and R^iπ_*-of-twists vocabulary that does not exist; stating it loosely would repeat the I-0118 false-statement trap. Recorded in I-0125.
18	- O(1) orientation convention (i→j transition multiplies by (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)^m) is documented but unvalidated until Γ(ℙⁿ, O(1)) is computed (T15 risk, flagged).
19	- v4.31 traps hit and recorded in memory: `attribute [local instance] MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` silently fails; notation3 can't capture universe binders; universe-pinning abbrevs break DT instance keys; the catToReflQuiver/Scheme.instCategory Quiver diamond blocks rw after `pullback.hom_ext`.
20	
21	## Why I stopped
22	Task not complete: deliverables 1–3 are done (3 fully proved, 2 modulo the one C2 leaf the task's named-sorry policy allows), deliverable 4 is half done — `sectionGradedModule_fg` stated, `quotBoundedness` deferred for lack of prerequisite vocabulary; remaining work (C2 proof, CM-regularity statement design) each need a fresh focused session and exceed remaining context.
23	
24	## Next
25	- Prove `twistTransition_cocycle` (C2): build `pullbackUnitIso` comp-coherence + scalar-atom pullback naturality, reduce to the (Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ)=(Xᵢ/Xₖ) identity in `Away 𝒜 (XᵢXⱼXₖ)`; this de-sorries the whole D2/D3 cone at once.
26	- Dedicated statement-design session for `TODO.quotBoundedness`: define CM-regularity + the R^iπ_* twist API against the Čech engine first.
27	- Then the QuotScheme restatement per I-0118 C-0002, at `Scheme.{0}`, consuming `IsProjectiveWith`.

data.content:
379	  finitely generated graded module over \(R(X_s, L_s)\): the twists
380	  \(\mathcal{F}_s \otimes L_s^{\otimes m}\) are globally generated for
381	  \(m \gg 0\), and the finitely many generating sections in low degrees together
382	  with the multiplication maps generate \(M(X_s, \mathcal{F}_s, L_s)\) over
383	  \(R(X_s, L_s)\). Finite dimensionality of each component over \(\kappa(s)\)
384	  follows from coherence and properness (finiteness of cohomology of coherent
385	  sheaves on a proper scheme over a field).
386	\end{proof}
387	
388	\begin{lemma}[Existence and uniqueness of the Hilbert polynomial of a rational
389	  graded series]
390	  \label{lem:hilbertPoly_exists_mathlib}
391	  \lean{Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly}
392	  \mathlibok
393	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
394	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Polynomial.HilbertPoly}).}
395	  Let \(F\) be a field of characteristic zero, let \(p \in F[X]\), and let
396	  \(d \in \mathbb{N}\). Then there is a unique polynomial \(h \in F[X]\) such that
397	  for some \(N \in \mathbb{N}\) and all \(n > N\) the coefficient of \(X^n\) in the
398	  power-series expansion of \(p \cdot (1 - X)^{-d} \in F\llbracket X\rrbracket\)
399	  equals \(h(n)\):
400	  \[
401	    \exists!\, h \in F[X],\ \exists N,\ \forall n > N : \;
402	    \bigl[ X^n \bigr]\bigl( p \cdot (1-X)^{-d} \bigr) \;=\; h(n).
403	  \]

text: The blueprint proof of `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` correctly has no `\leanok` (only the statement does) — consistent with the Lean `sorry`. Let me confirm axioms/build via lean_verify.

All verified. I have what I need.

## T14 Projectivity Foundation — Review

**Verdict: CONVERGING / honest.** Real, structural progress. Four new files land the projective-morphism vocabulary the whole Quot/Picard endgame needs. Exactly two `sorry`s, both named, typed, disclosed in the report and mirrored by blueprint nodes that do *not* claim proof-completeness. No hidden `admit`/axiom, no fake progress, and — critically — **no I-0118 false-as-pinned trap**.

### (a) Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: OK (1-to-1)
- `IsProjectiveWith π L` (ProjectiveMorphism.lean:75) encodes exactly `def:projective_with`: `∃ d, ∃ closed immersion i : X ↪ ℙ(Fin(d+1);S)` with `i ≫ (ℙ ↘ S) = π` (over S) and `L ≅ i^*O(1)`. `Fin(d+1) = {0,…,d}`, twist degree `1`. Faithful "projective + relatively very ample."
- Stability facts are genuinely PROVED (complete tactic proofs, no `sorry`/`admit` in ProjectiveMorphism.lean — grep-confirmed): `isProper`, `comp_isClosedImmersion`, `baseChange`. `lean_verify` on `baseChange` and `isProper` returns axioms `{propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` — the `sorryAx` is inherited *only* from the `twistingSheaf → serreTwist → twistTransition_cocycle` dependency, not from these proofs. This is accurately disclosed (report line 8; blueprint `def:serre_twist` STATUS comment). So the proofs are done; the cone is not yet axiom-clean, and the DAG shows this through the unproved cocycle node — honest.
- All 12 new-section nodes map cleanly: `def:projective_space`↔`ProjectiveSpace`, `gradeZeroRingEquiv`, `isProper_over`, `isPullback_map`, `awayUnit`, `twistTransition_cocycle`, `serreTwist`, `twistingSheaf`, `twistingSheafBaseChange`, `IsProjectiveWith(.isProper/.comp_isClosedImmersion/.baseChange)`. No mismatched or missing `\uses` edges found.

### (b) False-as-pinned risk: NONE
`sectionGradedModule_fg` (SerreFiniteness.lean:62) hypotheses: `π : X ⟶ Spec κ`, `[Field κ]`, `hproj : π.IsProjectiveWith L` (the very-ample/closed-immersion form), `hF : F.IsFinitePresentation` (coherent). Conclusion: section ring Noetherian ∧ section module `Module.Finite` ∧ each component `FiniteDimensional κ`. This is the **strong, true** Serre statement — projectivity, not proper-only. It is the deliberate inverse of the I-0118 trap, and the blueprint `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` was correctly flipped to hypothesise the very-ample `IsProjectiveWith` predicate (statement-`\leanok`). The one genuinely false-as-pinned statement in the chapter, `thm:quot_representable`, is untouched by T14 and correctly carries `\notready` + an explicit I-0118 caveat.

### (c) Blueprint proof-completeness
- `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` proof block has **no `\leanok`** (only the statement does) — correctly signals the Lean `sorry`. The math sketch is a faithful Serre-theorem outline. Good.
- `lem:serre_twist_cocycle` proof: also correctly lacks `\leanok`. The scalar identity `(Xᵢ/Xⱼ)(Xⱼ/Xₖ)=(Xᵢ/Xₖ)` is shown, but the actual hard part — the `pullbackUnitIso`/transport coherence that the Lean `sorry` covers — is asserted ("compose transitively... hence... as claimed") rather than argued. Slightly hand-wavy, but honest (proof not marked done). Minor.
- All other new-section proofs are complete and `\leanok`.

### (d) Missing references
- `def:projective_space`, `def:serre_twist`, `def:projective_with` each carry `\source{nitsure-hilbert-quot}`. Good.
- `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` cites Serre finiteness textually — [Nitsure] §1 and [Hartshorne] II.5.17 / I.7 (in both the Lean docstring and a `\textit{Source:…}`) — but has **no formal `\source{}` anchor**, even though `hartshorne-algebraic-geometry` exists in `references/manifest.yaml`. Recommend adding `\source{hartshorne-algebraic-geometry}` (and ideally anchoring the II.5.17 page). Same gap for the Serre-twist construction (only Nitsure anchored; Hartshorne II.5 / EGA II not `\source`-anchored). Minor, non-blocking.

### Deliverable coverage vs task
Deliverables 1–3 complete (3 fully proved modulo the one allowed cocycle leaf); deliverable 4 is **half**: `sectionGradedModule_fg` stated, but `quotBoundedness` (Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity) was deliberately *not* stated to avoid restating the I-0118 trap without the missing `Rⁱπ_*`-of-twists vocabulary. That deferral is a correct judgement, not avoidance — flagged in the report and I-0125.

No inbox issue warranted: the two `sorry`s are already tracked (I-0109 for `sectionGradedModule_fg`; C2 cocycle in the report/memory), and the only actionable nit — adding the Hartshorne `\source{}` anchor to `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` — is cosmetic.

Relevant files: `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/ProjectiveMorphism.lean`, `SerreFiniteness.lean`, `SerreTwist.lean` (sorry at line 322), `ProjectiveSpace.lean`; blueprint `/home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex` (`sec:projective_vocabulary` at line 5043, `lem:sectionGradedModule_fg` at line 330).
